fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oregon Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity oregon, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to oregon, speech-language pathologist compact oregon, oregon speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Oregon Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology
board.mailing_address:
Oregon Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
800 NE Oregon St, Suite 407
Portland, OR 97232
board.physical_address: 800 NE Oregon St., Suite 407, Portland, OR 97232

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Oregon.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Oregon eLicense portal, log in, and complete the online application form for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure. Pay the required application fee through the system.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official graduate transcripts, verifying a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Oregon Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

steps.title: Provide Praxis Scores or ASHA CCC Verification
steps.description: Submit official scores for the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology directly from ETS to the Board, or arrange for ASHA to verify your Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC) if you hold it.

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship Verification
steps.description: If you do not hold an ASHA CCC, complete and submit the Clinical Fellowship Report form. If you hold an ASHA CCC, arrange for ASHA to verify it directly with the Board.

steps.title: Undergo Fingerprint-Based Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete the fingerprinting process for a criminal background check.

documents.detail: Oregon State Police and FBI background check.

telepractice_text: Oregon permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Oregon is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Oregon issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Oregon does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does an Oregon SLP license cost?
Is Oregon part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Oregon allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid OR license.

faqs.question: Does Oregon have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Oregon?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Oregon does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Oregon
hero.dek: Apply for your Speech-Language Pathologist license in Oregon through the Oregon eLicense system, the state's online licensing portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the Oregon eLicense portal URL.
2. Click 'Register a New User Account' to create your profile.
3. Follow the prompts to enter personal information, set up security questions, and create a username and password.
4. Verify your email address, if required, to activate your account.
5. Once registered, log in to your account to begin the application for an SLP license.
